diagrams.title: Truth Table Structure for Two Variables
diagrams.description: Shows how to systematically fill truth table rows and columns for propositions p and q with all five logical connectives
elements: Column headers: p, q, ¬p, ¬q, p∧q, p∨q, p→q, p↔q
Row 1: T, T, F, F, T, T, T, T
Row 2: T, F, F, T, F, T, F, F
Row 3: F, T, T, F, F, T, T, F
Row 4: F, F, T, T, F, F, T, T
Pattern: alternate T/F in powers of 2 (1,2,4 rows for successive variables)
diagrams.purpose: Learn to construct and read truth tables correctly for any number of propositions

diagrams.title: Quantifier Negation Rules
diagrams.description: Visual diagram showing how to negate statements with universal and existential quantifiers using circle representations
elements: Arrow turning to ∃x ¬P(x) with one element marked 'not satisfying'
¬∃x P(x) shows empty inner circle turning to ∀x ¬P(x) with all elements marked 'not satisfying'
diagrams.purpose: Remember quantifier negation rules through visual transformation
